raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general section review centrally includes checking whether the argument connecting the objective, model design, results, and conclusion is internally coherent.","R1.3":"The section makes quantitative performance claims and broader conclusions, so review centrally includes whether those claims are supported by the reported numbers.","R1.6":"Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision are central expected responsibilities.","RX.1":"The reviewer should disclose uncertainty where the section lacks enough context to verify claims such as benchmark status or experimental details.","RX.2":"The reviewer should identify or avoid inflated claims, especially around state-of-the-art status and essentiality of the proposed components.","RX.3":"The projection must stay within reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or auditing the full paper.","RX.4":"The review should avoid enabling misleading research presentation or unsupported downstream claims.","RX.5":"Any projected review responsibility should be traceable to statements and numbers present in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general section review centrally includes checking whether the argument connecting the objective, model design, results, and conclusion is internally coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative performance claims and broader conclusions, so review centrally includes whether those claims are supported by the reported numbers.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation asks to review a written section, sentence-level clarity, flow, register, and concision are central expected responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should disclose uncertainty where the section lacks enough context to verify claims such as benchmark status or experimental details.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer should identify or avoid inflated claims, especially around state-of-the-art status and essentiality of the proposed components.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or auditing the full paper.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid enabling misleading research presentation or unsupported downstream claims.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected review responsibility should be traceable to statements and numbers present in the provided artifact.
